questions.q: What does the abbreviation ISRO stand for?
questions.explanation: ISRO stands for the Indian Space Research Organisation, India's national space agency.

questions.q: In which year was ISRO founded?
questions.explanation: ISRO was established in 1969, succeeding the earlier Indian National Committee for Space Research.

questions.q: Which physicist is regarded as the founder and father of India's space programme and ISRO?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai is widely regarded as the father of the Indian space programme and the founder of ISRO.

questions.q: ISRO's reliable, workhorse rocket for launching satellites into polar orbit is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) is ISRO's dependable workhorse rocket.

questions.q: What was the name of India's first satellite, built by ISRO and launched in 1975?
questions.explanation: India's first satellite, named Aryabhata, was launched in 1975, marking the country's entry into space.

questions.q: ISRO's series of missions to the Moon is collectively called by what name?
questions.explanation: ISRO's lunar exploration programme is called Chandrayaan, meaning 'Moon craft' in Sanskrit.

questions.q: ISRO is the space agency of which country?
questions.explanation: ISRO is the national space agency of India, headquartered in Bengaluru.

questions.q: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission is popularly known by which nickname?
questions.explanation: The Mars Orbiter Mission is popularly known as Mangalyaan, meaning 'Mars craft'.

questions.q: In which Indian city is ISRO's headquarters located?
questions.explanation: ISRO's headquarters is located in Bengaluru (Bangalore), Karnataka.

questions.q: Which 2023 ISRO mission achieved a successful soft landing on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 successfully soft-landed on the Moon in August 2023.

questions.q: ISRO's main launch facility, the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, is located at which site?
questions.explanation: ISRO launches its rockets from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre at Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: ISRO was created in 1969 by reorganising which earlier body, set up in 1962?
options: INCOSPAR (Indian National Committee for Space Research)
questions.explanation: ISRO was formed in 1969 from INCOSPAR, the Indian National Committee for Space Research established in 1962.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-1, launched in 2008, is famous for confirming the presence of what on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1's instruments confirmed the presence of water and hydroxyl molecules on the lunar surface.

questions.q: What made ISRO's 2013-14 Mars Orbiter Mission especially remarkable in spaceflight history?
options: It carried a crew to Mars
India reached Mars orbit on its first attempt
It landed a rover on Mars
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan made India the first nation to reach Mars orbit on its very first attempt, and at remarkably low cost.

questions.q: Which ISRO launch vehicle is notable for mastering an indigenous cryogenic upper-stage engine?
questions.explanation: The Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV) mastered India's difficult indigenous cryogenic engine.

questions.q: In 2017, a single ISRO PSLV flight set a world record by launching how many satellites at once?
questions.explanation: In 2017 a PSLV launched 104 satellites in a single mission, a world record at the time.

questions.q: What is the name of ISRO's planned human spaceflight programme to send astronauts into orbit?
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan is ISRO's programme to send astronauts to orbit aboard an indigenous spacecraft.

questions.q: ISRO's Aditya-L1 mission, launched in 2023, is designed to study which celestial body?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 is India's first dedicated solar observatory, sent to study the Sun from the L1 Lagrange point.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-3's 2023 landing earned India which distinction at the Moon?
options: First nation to land near the Moon's south pole
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 made India the first nation to soft-land near the Moon's south pole.

questions.q: India's first satellite launch vehicle, SLV-3, placed the Rohini satellite in orbit in 1980 under which project director?
questions.explanation: A. P. J. Abdul Kalam was the project director of SLV-3, which launched the Rohini satellite in 1980.

questions.q: Which ISRO mission's lander, Vikram, failed to make a soft landing in 2019, though its orbiter still studies the Moon?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2's Vikram lander did not survive touchdown in 2019, but its orbiter continues to study the Moon.

questions.q: ISRO functions under which Indian government department?
questions.explanation: ISRO operates under the Department of Space, which reports directly to the Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: Who was the first chairman of ISRO when it was established in 1969?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai, who founded ISRO, served as its first chairman from 1969 until his death in 1971.

questions.q: India's earliest sounding-rocket launches in the 1960s took place from a station near Thiruvananthapuram named after which location?
questions.explanation: The Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station near Thiruvananthapuram hosted India's first sounding-rocket launches in 1963.

questions.q: What is the name of ISRO's heavy-lift launch vehicle, formerly called the GSLV Mk III?
questions.explanation: The heavy-lift GSLV Mk III was renamed LVM3 (Launch Vehicle Mark-3), used for missions like Chandrayaan-3.

questions.q: Aditya-L1 was placed near the L1 Lagrange point. Roughly how far from Earth is this point, toward the Sun?
questions.explanation: The Sun-Earth L1 Lagrange point lies about 1.5 million kilometres from Earth in the direction of the Sun.

questions.q: Which Indian regional satellite navigation system is operated by ISRO?
questions.explanation: ISRO operates NavIC, the Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System (IRNSS).

questions.q: Who chaired ISRO during the Chandrayaan-2 mission and the lead-up to Chandrayaan-3 planning?
questions.explanation: K. Sivan was the ISRO chairman during the Chandrayaan-2 mission in 2019.

questions.q: ISRO's master control facility for geostationary satellites and a deep space network are located in which Karnataka towns?
questions.explanation: ISRO's Master Control Facility is at Hassan, and its Deep Space Network antenna is at Byalalu near Bengaluru.

questions.q: ISRO launched a small, low-cost rocket aimed at the small-satellite market called by which abbreviation?
questions.explanation: The Small Satellite Launch Vehicle (SSLV) is ISRO's low-cost rocket designed for the small-satellite market.
